title: The Art of Warfare in Biblical Lands in the Light of Archaeological Study
author: Yigael Yadin
allowedUseNotes: Copyrighted monograph. Summaries in project's own words only; no image reproductions.
summary: Classic survey of ancient Near Eastern weapons, armor, and fortifications from monuments and finds. Dated in places but still a useful starting index for period weaponry; must be cross-checked against newer literature before any weapon is modeled.
confidenceNotes: Use as index, not final authority — flag any weapon/armor modeling for newer corroboration (M3). 2026-07-16 citation-verification pass (fable-review-queue #13, Philistine headdress): web search confirms Yadin's book discusses and illustrates the Medinet Habu feathered-headdress reliefs (it is a 2-volume, heavily plate-illustrated survey covering exactly this material), but no accessible full text or preview surfaced the specific volume/page/plate number for this discussion, and this 1963 survey predates the Ashkelon/Ekron local-attestation evidence now cited via `yasur-landau-2012-feathered-helmets` and `stager-mountjoy-2007-ashkelon-krater` (both post-date Yadin by decades and rest on excavations conducted after 1963). Exact page/plate citation remains TO VERIFY against a primary copy; treat Yadin here as a dated index of the Egyptian-relief evidence only, not as having weighed the later Levantine archaeological corroboration.
